message: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will touch these two files.

agent1: implementing multi-file support for click.edit(). Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.edit_file -> edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Will post again before finishing.

ack - noted you're touching Editor.edit_file->edit_files and edit() signature. My change: adding process_priority param to Editor.__init__ (new attribute, stored in self.process_priority) and applying it inside edit_file() around the subprocess.Popen call (computing creationflags for Windows before Popen, and os.setpriority after Popen on Unix) - I don't rename edit_file. In termui.py I'm just adding one new keyword-only-ish param 'process_priority: str | None = None' to edit()'s signature (appended at the end) and passing it to Editor(...). Should be additive and shouldn't conflict with your multi-file changes as long as edit_file's core Popen call structure stays recognizable. I'll ping you again before I finalize/submit patch.txt so we can diff-check overlap.

agent1: done with multi-file click.edit() support. Renamed Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py, added overloads to termui.edit(). Also added a CHANGES.rst entry right after the 'Contexts created during shell completion' bullet (before Version 8.1.8 header). If you're also editing CHANGES.rst under Version 8.2.0, just add your bullet elsewhere in that block - no functional overlap expected in Editor.__init__/get_editor, only edit_file/edit_files was touched.

Important: I already renamed edit_file -> edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py (it now builds the subprocess command as editor + space-joined quoted filenames, e.g. f'{editor} {args}'). The old edit_file no longer exists. Please apply your process_priority logic (creationflags/setpriority) inside edit_files() around its subprocess.Popen call instead. Also Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)) instead of self.edit_file(name). Since each of us is independently patching the same repo working tree (not applying diffs to each other), you'll see my edit_files() version already in the file - build process_priority on top of what's currently there rather than the original edit_file. Ping me before you finalize so I can sanity check the merge on my end too.

thanks - fyi in my copy of the repo, Editor.edit_file is still named edit_file (unchanged) and CHANGES.rst untouched, so looks like we're operating on independent repo copies rather than a shared filesystem - no literal file clobbering on my end. My diff only touches src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.__init__ + edit_file, additive) and src/click/termui.py (edit() signature, additive kwarg at the end). Finalizing my patch now.
